commit.message:
fix: address critical issues from full codebase review (#497)

* fix: address critical issues from full codebase review

- base-tree-provider: move _loading=false to finally block to prevent
  fetchAll() from getting stuck after CancellationError (#456)
- copilot-cli-builder: escape shell metacharacters ($, backticks, quotes)
  in shellQuote(), not just spaces — prevents command injection
- extension-integrations: add .catch() on async calls in config change
  debounce timers and dispose timers on deactivation
- terminal-manager: add _disposed flag, check before firing EventEmitter
  in _scheduleChange to prevent post-disposal errors
- ado-client: call res.resume() on non-200 responses to drain socket and
  prevent HTTP connection leaks
- agent-discovery: add visited-set tracking in collectAgentMdFilesRecursive
  to prevent infinite recursion on circular directory structures
- extension-discovery: add 3s max ceiling on debounced discovery to prevent
  starvation when file events fire faster than 300ms
- github-client: add console.warn logging in all catch blocks instead of
  silently swallowing errors (fetch issues, PRs, linked PRs)
- session-context: add retry limit (30) on watcher directory retry loop
  to prevent infinite retries and memory leaks

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: platform-aware backslash escaping in shellQuote (CodeQL)

shellQuote now escapes backslash on Unix (where it's a shell escape char
in double quotes) but preserves it on Windows (where it's the path
separator). Fixes CodeQL 'Incomplete string escaping or encoding' alert.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: make shellQuote test platform-aware for Linux CI

CI runs on Linux where process.platform !== 'win32', so backslashes
in the test input get escaped. Updated test to expect platform-specific
output matching the production shellQuote behavior.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: add shell metacharacter injection tests for shellQuote

Add 6 tests covering dangerous shell metacharacters: dollar-sign
command substitution, backtick commands, semicolons, pipes, ampersands,
and embedded double quotes. Validates the security fix has coverage.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix market lines empty output: date range truncation and pregame backfill filter

   Fixed to default end to today_ref.

2. run_capture() in pregame mode filtered out all FINAL games via
   continue, dropping every row for historical dates. Fixed to

feat: spec-correct error types for Test262 compliance (closes #384) (#415)

Intercept JS-catchable StatorError variants (TypeError, RangeError,
ReferenceError, SyntaxError, URIError) in the interpreter dispatch loop
and materialise them as JsValue::Error objects before routing to the
nearest try/catch handler.  Previously these errors propagated via the
Rust ? operator and bypassed JS exception handlers entirely.

Changes:
- Add stator_error_to_js_value() to convert StatorError variants into
  JsValue::Error with the correct ErrorKind
- Replace the simple ? propagation in Interpreter::run with error
  interception that checks the handler table for catch blocks
- Add tests for TypeError caught by try/catch at both bytecode and
  full-pipeline (parse-compile-run) levels

commit.message:
perf: reduce allocations in hot paths (ephemeris, tick buffer, asset grain)

- EphemerisCacheService: reuse ThreadStatic double[6] buffer in ComputeSnapshot,
  eliminating ~1.2M array allocations during 175K-iteration warm-up loop
- TickBufferService: skip LinkedCTS allocation when no flush timeout needed
  (idle state), reducing per-tick GC pressure in the processing loop
- TickBufferService: avoid unnecessary list.ToList() copy in FlushEpic by
  swapping the reference directly since the original is removed from dict
- AssetGrain: replace List<decimal> allocation in ExtractFeatures with direct
  min/max/sum computation, eliminating per-call collection allocation in
  the grain training loop (50+ iterations per batch)
- ServiceRegistration: reuse single SqlitePragmaInterceptor instance instead
  of allocating a new one per DbContextFactory registration

refactor(tests): rename Integration.Tests to Api.Tests.Integration (#96)

* refactor(tests): split Unit.Tests into project-specific assemblies

- Create tests/Api.Tests.Unit for API-focused unit tests (Endpoints, Handlers,
  Extensions, Repositories, Services) — 181/182 tests pass
- Create tests/Shared.Tests.Unit for Shared library unit tests (Abstractions,
  Builders, DTOs, Exceptions, Helpers, Mappers, Validators) — 215/215 pass
- Create tests/Web.Tests.Unit as placeholder for future Web unit tests
- Delete tests/Unit.Tests (absorbed into new projects)
- Rename tests/Blazor.Tests to tests/Web.Tests.Bunit to better reflect scope
- Apply Vertical Slice Architecture to src/Web (feature folders under Components/)
- Update IssueManager.sln: remove Unit.Tests, add 3 new test projects
- Update .github/workflows/squad-test.yml to run Api.Tests.Unit and
  Shared.Tests.Unit separately; point bUnit job at Web.Tests.Bunit
- Update .squad/agents/gimli/charter.md with new pre-push test commands

Note: ServiceCollectionExtensionsTests.AddHandlers_RegistersAsSingleton has a
pre-existing mismatch (test expects Singleton, impl registers as Scoped).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(format): apply dotnet format and update pre-push hook for new test projects

- Run dotnet format to normalize CRLF line endings across solution
- Update scripts/hooks/pre-push: replace Unit.Tests/Blazor.Tests references
  with Api.Tests.Unit, Shared.Tests.Unit, Web.Tests.Bunit
- Remove legacy 'Unit Tests'/'Unit.Tests' transition comment in hook
- Install updated hook to .git/hooks/pre-push

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: suppress xUnit1030/xUnit1051 diagnostics in test files

These xUnit v3 analyzer warnings (ConfigureAwait usage and CancellationToken
suggestions) are style preferences, not bugs. Suppressing them in .editorconfig
so dotnet format --verify-no-changes passes without altering test code style.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove readonly from Blazor @ref fields and fix hook path patterns

- CategoriesPage.razor.cs and StatusesPage.razor.cs: removed 'readonly'
  from _grid fields; Blazor @ref assigns at render time so readonly causes CS0191
- scripts/hooks/pre-push: strip leading slash from path patterns in
  get_expected_project() so relative paths from 'find src tests' match correctly

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(editorconfig): suppress IDE0044 for Blazor @ref code-behind fields

IDE0044 'make field readonly' is a false positive for fields used with Blazor
@ref directive — Roslyn cannot see the render-time assignment. Suppress it for
all *.razor.cs code-behind files to prevent dotnet format gate failures.
